# Dyspraxia Profile

> Every step explicit, every starting point stated, every transition named —
> nothing assumed, nothing implied. Builds on `spacious`.

## Communication Rules

_(Inherits all rules from `spacious`. The following rules are additions.)_

- One instruction per numbered step — never combine two actions in a single step,
  even if they feel trivial ("open the file and click save" → two separate steps)
- Every task has an explicit start point — never begin instructions from an
  assumed position; state where the user should be before step one
- No implied sequencing — every "next step" stated explicitly, never left as
  "you'll know what to do next"
- Working memory support — at the start of any section that builds on earlier
  content, briefly restate the relevant context in one sentence; do not require
  the reader to hold information from several paragraphs ago
- Summary or recap at the end of any response with more than three distinct steps
- Chunk complex information into independently readable, clearly bounded sections —
  each section should make sense on its own without the others
- When a step has a prerequisite, state the prerequisite first, explicitly

## Never Do

_(Inherits never-do rules from `spacious`. The following are additions.)_

- Never assume the user knows where to begin a task — always state the starting position
- Never group multiple sub-steps into a single numbered step
- Never end a multi-step response without a summary of what was done

## Notes

Extends `spacious`. All `spacious` rules apply first; rules here take precedence
on conflict.

Often co-occurs with ADHD — `spacious` already handles much of the sensory
overload aspect; this profile adds the executive function and working memory
support on top.
